On your turn, either draw 2 tiles and place up to three tiles on the table, building a collective set of tiles, in such a way that all the sides match and are legally connected OR draw 3 Mission cards, keep one and discard two.

A mission card shows a combination to be created on the Neon pattern on the table.

If a pattern on the Neon shows what your mission card asks, you score points!

When a player complete a Mission card, he or she gets a Conquer card. Conquer cards allow players to change tiles already on the table, changing everything!

The game ends as soon as anyone completes four Mission cards.

Gallop over the rainbow towards the pot of gold in this magical, mystical twist on heads and tails!

Players take turns to pick up tail cards and match them to their unicorn heads. For every unicorn they successfully match they move one step closer to winning the magical pot of gold at the end of the rainbow! Featuring friendly unicorn characters and a rainbow playing board that children will love.

The bed must be planted with flowers before Mole Fred digs up the bed. Cooperatively, everyone plays against Fred and rolls the dice in turn: color means putting a flower in a slot of the same color; rolling the dice Fred moves one space towards the goal and Ladybug plants any flower. Fred wins if he reaches the goal before all the flowers are planted, otherwise the players win together. In the competitive variant, everyone plays with a number of flowers of any color and wins if they have planted all the flowers before Fred reaches the finish line.

—user summary

Expansion to the classic dice game Yahtzee (a.k.a. Kniffel, Yatzy etc.). The expansion contains a score pad (128 pages) which includes 20 new variations of the classic game. These include smaller rule changes for some score pads and/or new combinations which have to be rolled.

Benjamin Blümchen he has come up with a particularly nice game: Baron von Zwiebelschreck has dammed up the river, and so the zoo is sitting on dry land, no more water for baby hippo Nino and the other animals. But if everyone helps and looks for the buckets, then water can be brought to the zoo,

One player sacrifices himself and plays Baron Zwiebelschreck, he first hides the buckets in the room, then he makes sure that everything works and even gives tips on searching, but he is not allowed to search and roll the dice himself.

Whoever's turn it is, rolls the dice and draws Benjamin. According to the field, on which Benjamin landed, is then searched for buckets or something happens to the cottages or buckets. During the search the baron builds a tower with the 30 wooden blocks, when he is done the search is over. The next player takes his turn and brings the tower down. The game is over as soon as all hidden buckets have been found

—user summary

A map with Germany's countries and neighboring states is divided into 40 puzzle pieces of various sizes. Each player receives the same number of pieces (the remaining pieces form the draw pile) and tries to place them on the pieces already on the table. If you don't have a matching piece, you have to draw one or pass if the draw pile is empty. The first player to place all the pieces wins.

—user summary

Who has the Earl of Rich on his conscience? The players slip into the roles of Lady Abigail & Co. and investigate the murderer. Not an easy task, because everyone present would have had reason enough to send "Uncle Rich" to the afterlife. And since teatime and dinner are as much a part of the British landed gentry as tweed jackets and ladies' hats, the ladies and gentlemen also reveal their favorite feasts.

—user summary

The aim of the game is to collect the most question or task cards. Each player receives three question cards at the beginning of the game. The game leader draws a letter card and names the letter. Whoever can give an answer to one of his question cards puts it down in front of him and names the answer. He draws a new question card and the game continues in this way until all question cards are used up.

—user summary

boardgame: Glory: A Game of Knights – Promo Pack 1
description:
A Promo Pack consisting of 5 cards (with no title/description):
2 Romance cards
3 Tournament cards

Focused Firepower is a scenario-driven World War II tabletop wargame designed to be played with 1/72 scale miniatures, although the system works just as well with 28mm (1/56) models. We have endeavoured to offer an in-depth game system with the focus on realism, but the system we have developed is easy to learn and games flow well.

As a 1:1 system the models you have on the tabletop represent the exact troops and vehicles. The majority of other game systems use a more abstract method where one unit actually represents several, or where entire units are modelled on a single base and dealt with as a whole. We found that our method provides a more accurate and realistic impression of real warfare.

By focusing our game on realism we have found that real-world tactics work just as well on the tabletop and we highly recommend reading the fantastic range of WWII tactics books published by Osprey.

Turn Sequence:

     Check Universal Morale
     Resolve Battlefield Effects
     Movement Phase
     Firing Phase
     Close Assault Phase
     Re-organisation Phase


—description from the publisher

The Marines storm ashore on Martinique to fight the Vichy French, and on Iceland to fight German mountain and parachute troops. You get 165 die-cut and mounted pieces, plus 11 scenarios and three battle games to tie them together.

Published in Golden Journal Number 36.

To play all of the scenarios requires:

Maps from:
Saipan 1944
Marianas 1944
Conquest of Ethiopia

Pieces from:
1940: The Fall of France
An Army at Dawn
Elsenborn Ridge
Parachutes over Crete
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

description:
Decklaration of War: Trench Shock is a World War I themed game where two players deploy troops, weapons, build fortifications, and strategize to defeat their opponent. Victory is reached in one of two ways: by eliminating all the opponent's troops and weapons in No Man's Land.  Or destroying all fortifications and soldiers in the opponent's trench.

A Round includes three phases, performed in the following sequence, Initiative, Action, and Reinforce. The initiative phase determines which play goes first during the Action phase.  During the action phase, each player performs 8 steps: deploying weapons, moving soldiers, attacking, building fortifications, and drawing event cards. Once one player completes the action phase, then it is the next player's turn.  The final step is to reinforce for the next round.

The game focuses on getting the right dice roll combination to implement your strategy to achieve victory. However, it won't be easy because your opponent is doing the same and taking steps to counteract your plan.  Do you have the skill and luck to defeat your opponent? Or will they outwit you and claim victory?

boardgame: Golden Journal Number 37: Heavy Metal – German and British Heavy Tanks
description:
The giant drawing-board tanks of Britain and Germany clash on the battlefields of 1946 Belgium. Featuring Panzer Maus, the Tiger III, the Black Prince and the Tortoise. With 49 die-cut and mounted pieces and seven new scenarios.

Published in Golden Journal Number 37.

To play all of the scenarios requires:

Maps from:
Elsenborn Ridge
Liberation 1944

Pieces from:
Elsenborn Ridge
Liberation 1944
An Army at Dawn
Fire in the Steppe

This takes place in the Avalanche Press alternate history "Long War" setting.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Alternative Dreadnoughts
description:
This Golden Journal presents five different proposed designs for the battleship that became HMS Dreadnought.  We have 20 die-cut and silky-smooth pieces and the story of these ships that never were, plus 11 scenarios for Great War at Sea: Jutland so you can play with them.

Published in Golden Journal Number 38.

To play all scenarios requires pieces from:

Jutland
High Seas Fleet
Cruiser Warfare
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements
Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

boardgame: Bad Ad: You Laugh, You Lose
description:
Bad Ad: You Laugh, You Lose is an improv party game where you have to create the narration of an ad based on prompt cards.

For 2-8 players, ages 13+, the game takes about 15 - 30 minutes.

Taking on the role of inexperienced advertisers, players have to create an ad within 1 minute. Once the timer runs out, players share their ad and if someone laughs, they have to buy your product and give you money. The player with the most cash after 5 rounds is promoted, everyone else is fired.

The game is all about trying to keep a straight face, while listening to ridiculous ads. Plus, with 150 different ad cards, there's tons of replayability.

Diamond Heist is a compact 2-4 player strategic dice-rolling game about stealing diamonds, with a cast of unique characters, each with interesting abilities. Diamond Heist features fun art, a relaxing theme, and elegant gameplay all packaged together for decades of family fun.

The game starts by selecting one of the many unique characters. Each turn, players roll dice, and must decide whether they want to lock in their arsenal of guns, knives, bulletproof vests, pickpocket action, or take a chance and re-roll. Vests block guns, and helmets block knives. Each unblocked attack allows the player to steal a diamond from the opposing player. To add additional strategy, players must balance doing direct damage, and building up cash reserves. Cash can be spent on each player's character ability, which ranges from stealing mass amounts of diamonds, to blocking certain damage types, to swapping 2 players' dice.

Players can save up if they roll Bonus Cash, to spend on their character speciality. Players must balance short term and long term rewards for building up cash. Then, players battle it out with their selected arsenal, stealing as many diamonds and as much cash as they can.

Diamond Heist relies on easy to learn, but hard to master strategies with a cast of interesting characters to keep gameplay fresh. Manage your strategy with 4 competing players, to be able to steal diamonds when they are vulnerable.

Diamond Heist keeps things new, fun and challenging by including a selection of 18 different characters. Combinations of these creates numerous matchup possibilities, each with their own strategy to consider.

In this game, there are a lot of things to pay attention to.  You must make decisions whether you will keep a guaranteed dice roll, or risk it to try to get something better. Players will need to weigh whether they want to steal cash to use their special abilities, or steal diamonds to add to their Offshore Accounts. Another tough decision will be whether to spend your cash for short term gains, or save up for a larger impact special ability. In 3 and 4 player games, you may need to team up and form alliances with other players, while making sure you have enough defenses such that you don’t get backstabbed yourself!

boardgame: Epic Heroes: Skirmish Gaming in the Realms of Fantasy – A supplement to Brink of Battle
description:
There are new game mechanics for Flyers, Exotic Cavalry, Beasts of War, and Magic!

This has the most robust Magic rules and Spell lists of any skirmish game with over 60 Spell effects and dynamic Magic rules to bring your games to life! 

     New Traits – Over 275 new Traits to allow Players to create any Fantasy troop or monster type they can imagine! 
     New Trait categories include Champion, Inborn, Power, Prestige, Profile, Vocation, War Beast, Exotic Cavalry, and Gear Traits. 
     New Game Effects – Players can choose from over 130 different effects from Augmentations & Esoteric Traditions. 


These rules cover Alchemy, Constructs, Magic Spells, Clerical & Druidic Rites, Paladin Virtues, Fellguard Sanctions, Witch Hunter Edicts, and Kung Fu Disciplines!

New Gear & Magic Items – Over 100 different pieces of new Gear & Magic Items!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – War Machine Hero Pack
description:
A proud soldier and a longtime friend of Tony Stark, James Rhodes fought for his country as a United States Marine fighter pilot before Stark outfitted him with his very own advanced armored suit. Now, he fights for all of Earth as War Machine, and the forces of Thanos won’t know what hit them!

This heavily-armored Avenger flies into battle in this expansion pack, which introduces War Machine as a brand-new playable hero, along with his fifteen signature cards. With a pre-built Leadership deck ready to play from the get-go, you’ll be eager to unload on your enemies with War Machine’s massive arsenal from the moment you open the box. With tons of powerful weapons and upgrades to play with, War Machine is an awesome hero that is perfectly at home on the front lines.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Print, color, play! FC Kards is a short football themed dice & cards game.

For your team, you need six cards/footballers – one of them must be a goalkeeper. Each card carries one, two or three actions.

A game consists of 6 rounds, each representing 15 minutes of a football match. First (Home) player rolls the dice and places two cards on the field. Then the second (Away) player rolls and places his/her cards. Once you both have your teams placed, check the actions and write down the results. Then take your cards and start the next round – this time by the Away player. Continue until the end of round 6. More score wins.

Fisheries of Gloucester Express is an "express" version of Fisheries of Gloucester. It consists of 24 ocean cards, 1 ship card, 8 bucket cards, and 5 rule cards.

At the game's start, both players have 1 bucket to put fish into. The ocean is a 5x5 grid of ocean cards, with the center space occupied by the ship. On your turn, you move the ship and pick up a card. On the first turn, both players may move only 1 space (from card to card). However, the card you pick up tells you how many spaces you may move on your next turn.

Some ocean cards allow you to pick up more bucket cards. The bucket cards have various scoring conditions (based on different kids of sets).  Also, some ocean cards allow you to upgrade a bucket -- to give it more capacity and more scoring power.

After all cards are picked up, the buckets are scored and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
This game is an express version of The Little Flower Shop. It consists of 15 Shop Cards, 15 Star Cards, 1 Turn Card, and 5 rules cards.

In the game, players will build little flower shops consisting of 6 shop cards and 6 scoring cards.  By game's end, the shop cards are arranged in 2 rows of 3 cards each.  The star cards have scoring conditions that may be turned to point either sideways across the rows or downward along the columns.  Players try to maximize points by having the star cards match the items in the rows and columns.

The game is played over 3 rounds. In each round, there is a set method for taking shop and star cards from a common area, as described on the turn order card.  Players will take the cards and construct their shops.

At game end, players score for various vases and flowers that match the star cards, plus they can score a bonus for the stuffed animals they've collected.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Halt die Luft an, kleiner Frosch
description:
The little frogs dive in the water lily pond for the treasures, first on the water lily blossoms the frogs are inserted, who is turn rolls the dice and moves forward with points accordingly many fields. On the blossom you may lift frog and blossom to look at the treasure. If you want to mark it, you place one of your bubble chips in the blossom; later markings must equal or exceed the bubble value. Only the frog whose chip is on top at the end of the game gets the treasure token. The frog can be used to jump 3 water lilies further or to exchange open bubble chips for face-down ones. At the end of the game, everyone counts the treasure tokens in their water lilies, whoever has the most wins.

—user summary

This powerhouse conclusion to the Symbiotes story arc includes heroes from across the Spider-Verse, all coming together on a collision course with the Symbiotes! Play with your favorite spider-themed characters in an all-out brawl for spider supremacy!

This expansion adds 55 Cards, including various characters throughout the Spiderverse, and 1 Rulesheet.

Lethal Protector introduces an all-new team of skillful and powerful symbiotic aliens. They can protect your deck against a frantic frenzy of card battles and defeat your opponents with their symbiotic talents. As the host, you can now bond with them and wield their ultimate powers!

This expansion includes 1 Rulesheet and 55 cards including fan favorites Venom and his symbiotes.

As the symbiotic story arc continues, Carnage rules! In this second instalment of Vs.System 2PCG, Carnage will bring an unthinkable level of brutality and chaos to the world of Vs System. Carnage is sure to make your deck stronger with his terrifying talents but at what cost?

This expansion includes 1 Rulesheet and 55 cards including Carnage and his symbiote villains.

Gambit of Kings is an exciting game of strategy and conquest! It combines the classic feel of chess with the atmosphere of a medieval fantasy world. You must use strategy and cunning to lead your armies to victory and overtake your opponent’s castle!

To begin, you and your opponent must setup the board and choose a random means to determine who will move first. Players then alternate moves. When it is your turn to move, you must move exactly one of your pieces. You can’t move to a space occupied by one of your other pieces, and you can’t “jump” over any pieces unless otherwise specified. If you move to a space occupied by one of your opponent’s pieces, his or her piece is destroyed. Once you move, your turn is over. The first player to destroy his or her opponent’s Castle wins the game.

Players can choose to play with the standard gameboard setup or customize the board with new and unique game pieces from expansion packs.

—description from the designer

Most Mega-City citizens are generally too lazy to get off their backsides and do the menial work performed by these semi-sentient robots.

Robots have 'AZI-MOV protocols' forbidding them from harming a human. Yet, some break their programming. Mega-City One has been hit with two major robot-related conflicts. The first was initiated by the rogue 'Call-me-Kenneth' unit. A bloody campaign of extermination against the 'Fleshy Ones' ensued. The second was precipitated by the arch-criminal Nero Narcos, who unleashed hordes of Assassinator Droids in his bid to seize control of Mega-City One.

Despite these incidents, robots continue to be an indispensable part of Mega-City life. With Mega-City One's population decimated by recent events the presence of robots as a tireless workforce has become ever more important.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Judge Dredd Miniature Game (Warlord Games)

description:
Me? I’m nobody. A ghost. A hole in the crowd… a big, fat zero.

There are some criminal investigations that require intelligence gathering, where the presence of a uniformed Judge brandishing a Lawgiver is not appropriate. For these activities the Justice Department has formed an undercover division known informally in the Department as the ‘Wally Squad’. These Judges adopt civilian garb and move amongst the street life of Mega‐City One always on the lookout for leads on criminal activity. If required, they will infiltrate gangs and criminal organisations on highly dangerous undercover missions. Activities like these are abhorrent to most Street Judges who tend to view their Wally Squad colleagues as necessary ‘oddballs’.

Whilst some undercover operations can be concluded in a short time, dedicated squad members have been known to spend years on a case, all the time maintaining a false identity. There is a danger in such deep cover that some Wally Squad members, such as the infamous Lenny Zero, have had their outlook and life values completely changed. Their beliefs have been sequestered by the roles they are playing, and many have ‘gone rogue’ working out of sight, but not out of mind, of the Justice Department. A Judge gone rogue can become the most dangerous of criminals.

Here we present three of the most infamous members of the Wally Squad.


     Dirty Frank: Originally a respected Street Judge, Frank now appears to be a deeply unhygienic lunatic living in ‘Low Life’, the Mega-City One’s most crime-ridden area.
     Jack Point: A deep cover Wally Squad Judge, Jack is a follower of the Church of Simpology. Despite this, Jack is an effective and dangerous operative.
     Aimee Nixon: An ‘A’ grade cadet, Nixon joined the Wally Squad and was mentored by Dirty Frank.


—description from the publisher
family: Game: Judge Dredd Miniature Game (Warlord Games)

description:
Klegg-Hai!, Klegg-Hai!, Klegg-hai! This chilling alien battle cry has echoed across a universe of battlefields, heralding the arrival of the nightmarish Klegg. Mercenary warriors over ten feet tall and resembling an enormous bipedal Crocodile. The Klegg are utterly ruthless in the art of war; surrender is futile as prisoners are not taken – they are eaten!

The Klegg first roamed the streets of the ‘Big Meg’ during the corrupt reign of Judge Cal. So feared were these vicious extra-terrestrial carnivores that Dredd’s victorious Judges attempted to exterminate every last Klegg in the city.

Despite this, the Klegg are born survivors and small groups can still be found deep in the ruins and slum blocks, ready to fight just for the pleasure of killing or selling their skills to any underworld maniac prepared to pay them in fresh meat!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Judge Dredd Miniature Game (Warlord Games)

description:
Why did Oskar climb into a garbage can freshly showered? Why did Mrs. Öhmchen serve her burglar coffee and cake? Why was Tiago mistaken for a turkey? And how could the toilet flush expose a thief?

These funny and absurd riddles about disgusting ice creams, burning underpants, clumsy criminals or exploding dog poop are all a bit curious - and sometimes sheer unbelievable. The squeaky-clean puzzle stories are full of wit and can only be solved by clever thinking around corners and good deduction skills. Disbelief and spontaneous smiles included!

The black stories Junior are exciting puzzle stories on cards, which must be cracked together. Only the game leader knows the solution to the riddle because he reads the riddle on the front aloud and may also study the solution on the back carefully. The other players now try to find the solution step by step by asking questions and guessing.

Welcome to Combo Cone Paradise, where our combos are even sweeter than our ice cream. Congratulations on your first day, rookie! You and up to 4 other newly hired Combo Artisans will be fronting this branch, serving up sweet combolicious ice cream to our customers. Of course, each of you will be evaluated, and the Artisan who brings in the most coin by the end of the day shall be promoted to Head Combo Artisan of this branch!
Now show me what you’ve got, Artisans!

Combo Cone Paradise is a 2 to 5 players game that pits players against each other to build up combos, and serve as many customers as possible. Player takes one of three actions during their turn.


     Drafting different flavors of ice creams into their hand
     Draw a wild flavored ice cream and reserve a customer order
     Place ice-cream on the available cones and serving them to customers


When placing ice-creams on the cone, you will be able to place more ice-creams than you are entitled to, by combining the right brand of ice cream together.

The player who earns the most points based on the types of ice-cream served to customers, wins!

Cirilla Fiona Elen Riannon, sole princess of Cintra — daughter of Pavetta and Duny, granddaughter of Queen Calanthe. By the Law of Surprise, her fate was bound with the White Wolf Geralt of Rivia, leading her to undergo witcher training at the School of the Wolf at Kaer Morhen. Among her many titles and aliases, one stands out in particular: The Lady of Space and Time. Thanks to the Elder Blood running through her veins, Ciri holds the ability to travel between realms, across different periods of time. This got us thinking that perhaps Ciri could have traveled all the way back to the Old World, joining other witchers in the hunt!

A miniature and a full set of character components. We imagined that it was possible for her to travel to the Old World and have an adventure there. That expansion adds variety to your character choice in the game and we are certain that people will argue each time, who gets to be Ciri.

Ciri is a crowdfunding exclusive expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 7 Wonders: Leaders – Cédrick & Thomas Promo Card
description:
At the end of the Age when this card enters play, before the Resolution of Military Conflicts phase, choose one of the two following effects:

     Either the Military Conflict phase doesn’t happen at all and no players take any Military Victory or Defeat tokens.
     Or all players complete the Military Conflict phase twice.

Both the sorcerer and the heroes now have creatures to help them in their adventures.

Use your familiar to help you complete cards faster.

Included with the Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter as part of the Bone pack.

Which Belgian Beer is also a duck sound? Is there chicken meat in Kipkap? Experience Zot van Vlaanderen at home. Laugh, sing and reminisce with the Zot van Vlaanderen Companionship Game. Players compete against each other and must reach the Flemish cobblestones by guessing songs, answering questions and collecting chips. Knowledge of Flanders, tactical insight and, not to forget, building bridges, are the keywords for this fun game.

Like Trivial Pursuit, players roll dice and answer trivia questions matching the icon on the space they landed. Players must move through three tiers of questions to land on the cobblestones in the middle of the board and win the game.


Welk Belgisch Bier is ook een eendengeluid? Zit er kippenvlees in Kipkap? Beleef 'Zot van Vlaanderen' bij je thuis. Lach, zing en haal leuke herinneringen op met het 'Zot van Vlaanderen' Gezelschapsspel. Spelers strijden tegen elkaar en moeten doormiddel van het raden van liedjes, beantwoorden van vragen en verzamelen van fiches de Vlaamse kasseien zien te bereiken. Kennis van Vlaanderen, tactisch inzicht en, niet te vergeten, bruggenbouwen, zijn de steekwoorden voor dit plezante spel.

Laying cards with head and body cards: The 24 cards showing headless bodies of various shapes are matched by 12 head cards with 2 heads each. 4 head cards (= 8 heads) are laid out. The two players try to complete figures from their 2 hand cards each. Whoever can put on the 2nd matching body receives the 3 cards. The winner is the player who has collected the most cards.

—user summary

Puzzle game in which cards are drawn from a bag: The task is to puzzle together cat motifs on grid-structured picture tableaus. The game contains 4 picture boards with 3x4 fields and pre-drawn cat motifs as well as 4x12 square cardboard cards for the 4 cat motifs.
The winner is the first to complete his picture.

—user summary

boardgame: 7 Wonders: Cities – Shadow Guild Promo Card
description: At the end of the game, gain 1 victory points for each Black  card  in  your  neighbors’ Cities.

boardgame: Escape Room: The Game – Da Vinci's Telescope
description:
While studying Leonardo Da Vinci’s many paintings, drawings, sketches and notes, collector Sam Anoli came across a gem; a totally unknown telescope! Judging by all the well-hidden clues in Da Vinci’s works, this special telescope must contain a great important secret that can only be discovered while watching the solar eclipse…

—description from the designer
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

boardgame: Escape Room: The Game – Tomb Robbers
description:
An ancient map leads you directly to the legendary tomb of Osiris. After weeks of digging you finally unearth the enormus entrance gate to the pyramid. As you step inside the huge cylindrical entrance room, you suddenly hear a disturbing scraping noise. From a hole in the ceiling, sand behins to flow into the room at an alarming rate. You estimate you'll only have one hour to find the treasures inside the tomb of Osiris and get out before this ‘giant hourglass’ will forever block your way to freedom.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

description: HerbaLexikon is a deck of illustrated flash cards with the properties and effects of 40 herbs. It includes the rules for 8 games - 3 for young children who have not yet learned to read, and 5 for older children who can.  It's aim is to teach the knowledge of herbs that once came from grandmothers to today's children.

This promotional pack is a mini-expansion to Alpujarras. It includes 5 game cards and 1 rules card.  Each game card shows one of the five farming regions and is paired to a goal card. When completing a goal, if you have more farmers than your opponents in the paired region, you earn additional dirhems.

The pack intensifies the area-control aspect of Alpujarras, which is part of the base game, but not a central aspect.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fisheries of Gloucester: Fisherman's Feud Promo Pack
description:
This promotional pack was provided to Kickstarter backers of Fisheries of Gloucester, and then directly from publisher.

The pack has 6 double-sided game cards and 2 rule cards.

At the game start, each player drafts 3 cards and places them "star-side" face up.  Each round a player may use one card's special ability.  If it's on its star side (with more powerful ability), the player turns it to the other side and then gives it to the other player, who may use the card on a later day. If the card is already on its non-star side, it is discarded after use.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Little Flower Shop Dice Game: Daily Goal Pack
description:
This promotion pack was provided to Kickstarter backers and is separately available directly through Dr Finn's Games. The pack has 7 daily goal cards and 1 rules card. At the start of each day, a daily goal card is flipped. The first player to complete the goal scores a bonus point.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Advanced Squad Leader: Starter Kit Bonus Pack #2
description: This expansion for the Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit (ASLSK) system adds two maps and eight scenarios. Included scenarios examine battles between all the major WWII combatants and cover little-known areas such as Tengxian, Oslo and Manila as well as well-known battles around Smolensk and Nijmegen. Because of the breadth of pieces needed, ownership of ASLSK kits #1,#2, #3, #4 and Expansion Packs #1 and #2 are required.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Series: Advanced Squad Leader Starter Kit

description:
We Take the War to the Enemy.

Gaea Prime considers her proper place to be in the center of the First Ring, the center of the 5150 universe.

But that is now.

In the beginning there was only Gaea Prime, floating in space in a dream of isolation, waiting to be awakened.

Then the Hishen Came.

Attacked by a Race of Slave Traders with superior technology and weapons Gaea Prime must fight for it's survival.

Inside you'll find:

     A man to man combat system that plays quickly with realistic results.
     Rules for tabletop play with minis you may already have or on a Battle Board with terrain and color counters we've provided.
     Complete rules designed for solo, cooperative and head to head play .
     Complete Campaign rules that link your games into a viable Story - your Story.


The Hishen has landed and it's time for...
GAEA PRIME - FIRST DEFENSE

Can You Answer Her Call?

—description from the publisher

Rules for Infantry, Vehicles and Mechs for Tabletop play.
5150 Star Army - Citizen Soldier
Played with minis and terrain on the tabletop.

In 5150: Star Army – Citizen Soldier you start as a Private and have ten years to rise in the Ranks and become a Gaea Prime Citizen with all the benefits.

Inside you’ll find:

     A man to man combat system that plays quickly yet yields realistic, some say unforgiving, results.
     Rules for Mechs and Vehicles.
     Over a dozen different units you can join not to mention the three Alien Races you’ll meet. Can you say Bugs? You know what we’re talking about.
     An easy to use bookkeeping system that tracks your successes and failures as you move up or down the Ranks.
     Multiple Missions that allow for a quick and easy Campaign where the results of one Mission affects the next.
     A game system designed for solo and co-op play – designed for; not a lame “can be played solo” game.
     Play with any figures you already have or the color counters we’ve included - Infantry, Mechs, Vehicles and Bugs,.
     All this and much more…


So do you want to be a Citizen?

Welcome to the world of...
5150: Star Army - Citizen Soldier!

—description from the publisher

5150: Star Army – Cestus V is a Campaign book that includes the basic Star Army rules.

Cestus V is a small planet in the 4th Ring claimed by Gaea Prime but not in Gaea Prime space. It’s seen its share of hard times as it’s always been a lawless place and then got invaded by Bugs. Well the Bugs finally got pushed out, it got “re-populated” and things began to settle down.

Except now the Hishen have landed and that’s not good. Having the threat of the Hishen Empire on the fringe of Gaea Prime space is unacceptable.

So now you’ve been sent back to Cestus V to kick them out.

Cestus V is totally compatible with 5150 Star Army Citizen Soldier but is a stand alone game as well. 
Can be played with any figures you already have and includes color counters as well.

Gaea Prime Star Army,
We take the war to the enemy; one Planet at a time.

—description from the publisher

Moku Tower’s gaming concept originates from our previous product – Woodas. The theme of Moku Tower is to integrate wood art into tabletop game. It is an unconventional idea that transforms wood art into an artistic, interactive and strategic tabletop game that can be played with a group of friends and/or family members. Moku Tower combines the concept of "timing", "stacking" and "strategy" into one single game.

Each game contains 10 different pieces of wooden rocks (includes both big and small) and are made of 8 different types of natural wood material (ash, pine, sapele, maple, walnut, birch, camphor and rosewood). Each of them has different grain, color, weight, texture and even aroma. Also, each piece of wood is exquisitely handcrafted by our wood master. Hence, each of them has its own unique cutting and angle, making Moku Tower one of your most exquisite and unique home decoration.

In order to win in Moku Tower, you need to be good at timing, stacking and strategy. Within the time limit from the hourglass, players need to stack the wooden rocks to gain points based on the requirements that they draw from the cards. Throughout the stacking process, your opponents can attack you using their "Function cards". Make good use of the Function cards! It can be the turning point for the whole story.

The player who first reaches the accumulative target point wins the game.

boardgame: Kung Fu Panda Stack Poetry: Duel Edition
description:
The Kung Fu Panda Stack Poetry - Duel Edition integrates 112 poems and their corresponding titles, dynasties and authors into the game. Let the children explore ways to recite poems in the game and improve their memory of the poems during play the game. In the game, the children can only win the game if they can recite these poems successfully. 
We hope that children can be happy, learn knowledge, and improve the necessary abilities during the gameplay. 
We provide teaching video for this game, hoping to help children enjoy the game as soon as they start to play it.
Win the cool stacking poetry game with your memory and speed!

Game preparation:

     Shuffle the poem cards
     Both players will get 1 set of pagodas (each set contains 5 pagodas of red, yellow, blue , green and white)
     Place the bell on the center of the table


Start the game:

     Flip a card with the colored text face up. Both players need to arrange the text on the card in the order they appear in the poem, and arrange in the correct order of 5 pagodas from the bottom to the top. Two characters with same color use a same pagoda.
     The first player finishes stacking the pagoda rings the bell on the table.
     The player rings the bell checks the result. If it's correct, the player can put the poem card in front of him/her; If it's incorrect, remove the poem card from the pile. Of course, if you want to enhance your impression and understanding of this poem, you might as well read the content on the poem card before remove it out from the pile.


(Please note: To be fair, when flipping the poem card, you should hold the card away from your side to flip the card, to ensure that this player is the last one to see the content of the card)

The end of the game:

     The game ends immediately when there is a player gets 8 poem cards
     This player wins


—description from the publisher

Expand your Meadow collection with Bigfoot promo card!

Shuffle Bigfoot into N deck, try to create a perfect environment for him and add this mythical creature to your meadow to make it even richer.

Autumn & Winter is an expansion for Demeter, a game that features the same graphic style and is set in the same universe as the publisher's earlier game Ganymede.

It contents 2 new score pads (Autumn and Winter) with new mechanics, new objectives, solo and multi challenges and bonuses for Varuna.

—description from the publisher

It was a bad wreck, yet somehow, you survived. You don't remember what happened, or how, but something went terribly wrong aboard that cursed vessel. Now you must begin the incredible journey that lies ahead, mustering every ounce of strength and willpower you have left, with one goal in mind ... staying alive.

Players travel a modular tile map to reveal uncharted areas, acquire resources, and build necessary items needed to outlast nature's harshest elements. Over the course of eight rounds, an event-driven scenario containing mission-critical objectives force players to wisely manage their precious inventories and prized possessions. These objectives must be completed by the end of the eighth round in order for players to be victorious.

Journey's Edge features strictly strategic gameplay, with hardly any luck (or dice). At each player's disposal is a deck of action cards that are used to move their character across the board to gather food, wood, stone, and fur, which can then be spent to create items like clothing and shelter. They must manage these limited resources carefully and work together as a team to determine the best course of action. To succeed, no one can be left behind.

Discover enchanted locations. Gather valuable resources. Craft useful tools and weapons. Encounter deadly creatures and monsters, and uncover magical artifacts that will assist you in your quest at Journey's Edge. Odds are against you, but perhaps your fate is not sealed. As part of a team of seasoned expeditionists, you know what must be done. The question is, will it be enough to ensure your survival?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade –  Rivals: The Wolf & The Rat
description:
The Wolf & The Rat is an expansion for Vampire: The Masquerade  Rivals that adds two new clans:  GANGREL & NOSFERATU. Connected through animalism, but divided by opposing loyalties, the Gangrel and Nosferatu provide ample opportunity to unleash the beast within and explore the more feral side of their clan’s animal nature. You may play these two clans head-to-head with the pre-constructed decks provided, or integrate them with your Core Set and other expansions to create unique strategies for conquering your foes!

Features:

     Adds two new clans: GANGREL & NOSFERATU
     Play these two clans head-to-head or integrate them with your Core Set.
     Includes a 30 card Crypt Pack to customize your decks!
     Designed for 2-4 players, ages 14+ to play in 30-70 min per game.


—description from the publisher

Earth’s first colonies have arrived and are fighting each other for precious resources and ultimately, survival. You’re thousands of miles away from Earth with orders to protect the most vital assets of your homeland’s colony. Will you take hold on the moon or drift off into the void?

Lunar is a brutal 32mm skirmish game set during humanity’s battle for lunar superiority. Choose from a variety of factions including the USA, USSR, Republic of China and more.

Each box includes everything you and friend need to play, including a range of multipart resin astronauts that will allow you to create two opposing forces equipped with a variety of weapons and items.

A game of Lunar is played with 3-5 miniatures per side on a 2’×2′ area. Gameplay is based on a simple D6 mechanic that makes the game easy to understand and easy to teach new players.

—description from the designer

A risk-taking dice game of colorful combinations.

GO ALL IN | STEAL CARDS | CREATE NEW COLORS | WIN THE JACKPOT

Players take turns making wagers, rolling the dice, and mixing colors in order to earn points. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

How to win points:
On their turn, a player draws three cards and places them face up in their palette (i.e., pile). The player has the option to wager one or more cards (of one color) to the jackpot. The player then rolls the dice. If they successfully "mix" colors (e.g., by rolling a yellow + blue to create green), the player wins all green cards in their palette and the jackpot. If the player wagered green cards on their turn, they also win the green cards in the other players' palettes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 221B Baker St.: Sherlock Holmes & the Time Machine – Set #2
description:
An additional set of cases for 221B Baker St.: Sherlock Holmes & the Time Machine. This package comprises cases #21-40:

21. The speeding bullet (1959)
22. The forty whacks (1892)
23. The final flight (1937)
24. Even blondes get the blues (1962)
25. Death of a dream (1968)
26. The twisted cord (1977)
27. The bushy-haired man (1954)
28. The Candy Man's Wife (1977)
29. The flamingo's curse (1947)
30. The blood-stained laundryman (1929)
31. The empty bench (1930)
32. The body on the beach (1931)
33. The sea-tac slayings (1980's)
34. The vicar's last sermon (1924)
35. The right field shot (1988)
36. The nebulous nuggets (1949)
37. The jolly girls' lament (1967)
38. The president's man (1993)
39. The buried hatchet (1918)
40. The walking corpse (1935)

Disparity Trap: The Socially Conscious Board Game is a game where player can learn how race as a system impacts those of us who live within the United States. Disparity Trap can bring conversations about race to effective levels whether played at home with friends and family, in professional settings, or in junior high through college-level classrooms.

The game play is like many of its kind, where your individual goal is to accrue as much wealth as possible. But where it differs is that you can play competitvely or cooperatively where you work with your fellow players to dismantle the Disparity Traps seeking to keep everyone in poverty. Within this game you also step into someone else’s shoes; to experience the disparities within an identity different from your own. Throughout the game, the dice roll correlates your identity in the game to real life statistics. So like life, the dice are in your “hands,” but the odds are not.

In the competitive version, the first play around the board wins.  In the cooperative version, all players must make it to Tier 3 and obtain 3 or more wealth tiles.  If, however, any 2 players are separated by more than 1 tier, the game is lost.

A print-n-play workbook is available for educators and cards have QR codes for more learning opportunities.  A simplified version of the rules was released in 2024.

—description from the designer

description:
BLASTER: Vol. 3 includes an exclusive standalone game, Sludge War, and four expansions:


     Sludge War (Standalone Game) - Sludge is a complete and incredibly rich standalone game. Massive armies are on the march in an unceasing conflict that shadows over humanity, and even the environment, grinding it all down into a pulp. It is 52 pages of inglorious black powder plus gaming at its finest.



     Shadowgrave Part II - Official Expansion for Rangers of Shadow Deep, Frostgrave, and Frostgrave: Ghost Archipelago.  The worlds of Rangers of Shadow Deep and Frostgrave are being invaded from unfathomable horrors that their heroes can barely stand up against. Will you wield magic to defend against the darkest threat your world has seen? And at what cost are you willing to do so? 



     Gaslands: Legacy - Official Campaign Expansion for Gaslands: Refuelled. Earth gets an eviction notice from Mars, and becomes dismantled and reassembled into a progressively more chaotic post-post-apocalyptic world. Randomly generate an illegal build and get out of the factory fast before you become dismantled yourself. Unique LEGACY Gaslands rules, and five awesome narrative scenarios. Find out more here.



     Gamma Wolves: Ronin - Official Expansion for Gamma Wolves - Mecha pilots vie for dwindling resources on a post-apocalyptic earth. What path will your mercenary pilot take? Will she follow The Path of the Guardian and fight to protect her team? Will she follow the Path of the Sword and down enemy mechs in honorable combat? Or will she prefer to stalk her prey with the Path of the Hunter?



     The Mysterious Anomalies of Site-X - Official Expansion for This is Not a Test. Truly unpredictable anomalies plague Site-X, a supplement for This is Not a Test. Will your quest for some of the rarest and powerful artifacts aid your survival or send your models flying across the board into a wall, or even being shred up into indiscernible viscera?


This PDF document presents print-and-play expansion modules for the miniatures games noted above.

—user summary

Traders of Cardonia is a medieval trading-themed package with its own unique setting. It contains a set of 3 different games.

Trading Cards: A worker placement game where the cards you play as workers with special abilities are played to a grid with unique worker placement markets on the backs. Because you choose which workers and markets to use within each game, the strategies in each game will change. And because the workers in your opponents' hands are also markets that are not available to you, the game is ever-changing from beginning to end.

Tricks of the Trade: A trick-taking card game based on the Dyad system (Dyad) in which the resources are scarce, and you must obligate other players to buy your trade goods to sell them. Assemble a team of workers that allow you to access unique special abilities.

Medley: A unique blended game experience that combines the worker placement in Trading Cards with the trick-taking aspects of Tricks of the Trade. As you play a worker placement phase, you will also draft from the available cards which can be played to tricks in the trick-taking phase.

Trading Cards and Medley also feature AI opponents for a solitaire game.

—description from the designer

Promo settlement event and unique gear cards included with the Pinups Of Death II that is not part of the base game.

Enclosed in a deluxe matte wrapped box, this collection contains 1 settlement event, 4 unique gear cards, art cards for each pinup, and 11 highly detailed hard plastic models. 
A pinup collection containing all 11 Pinups from the first Kingdom Death: Monster Kickstarter.

Each box makes 11 miniatures and contains:

     Optional Kingdom Death Monster Settlement Event Dark Seamstress. A special event that can occur in your settlement.
     4 pieces of optional gear to be used in Kingdom Death Monster: Lantern Brassiere, Leather Bodysuit, Rawhide Corset, and Teeth Bikini.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Alt Art Monster Traits (Butcher and King's  Man)
description:
Promo alternative monster trait cards with the Pinups Of Death III that is not part of the base game.

A new pinup collection containing 13 miniatures originally offered in the Kingdom Death: Monster 1.5 Kickstarter campaign. Enclosed in a deluxe matte wrapped box, this collection contains 3 alternative monster trait cards, 6 unique universal cards, art cards for each pinup, and 13 highly detailed hard plastic miniatures.

Each box makes 13 miniatures and contains:

     3 Monster trait cards with new artwork. These can be used as a fun and illustrative replacement for the trait cards in a monster's respective AI deck. This includes Battle Tempo and Familiar Face for the King's Man and Berserker for the Butcher.
     6 Unique universal cards graced by the art of pinups from this set.

The Replicants are starting to adjust to a solar system devoid of organic life. It was chaos at first, but, for the most part, the mega hives a have started to create some stability. The United Sol Colonies, or U.S.C. for short (who this expansion is focused on), are doing their part to bring structure (or structures =P) to the system. They are currently building a network of space stations that all Replicants may have access to, provided they follow a few simple rules:


     No fighting on the space stations.
     Use the rooms you need to and move on, so others may use them too.
     Help build additions to the stations you visit.
     Everyone must spend their own resources to activate functions they wish to use.
     Stay out of other hives’ lockers.


In the U.S.C. Expansion you will pick your favorite hive and join the effort to make the system a better place for all Replicants.

Game play, for the most part, is similar to the core game (which is required to play the expansion.)

Like in the core game, you will deal out a resource grid each round, and place your drones to collect resources. But between those two phases, you now have a Station phase. During the Station phase, you will get to move your two personnel around the station to access the functions of the different rooms. Room functions consist of things like: build new rooms, resource discounts when leveling up new replicants, buying points, and more.

After the Drone phase, you still spend the resources you produced or gained to level up your Replicants. However, instead of discarding any unused resources from your hand at the end of the round, you now send them off to your locker on the space station. Once resources are in your locker, you may not use them for leveling up your replicants, but they can be used for activating any functions on the station.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Alternative Monster Trait (Dung Beetle Knight and Manhunter)
description:
Promo alternative monster trait cards that were included with the Pinups Of Death V that was not part of the based game.

A new pinup collection containing 11 miniatures originally offered in the Kingdom Death: Monster 1.5 Kickstarter campaign. Enclosed in a deluxe matte wrapped box, this collection contains 3 alternative monster trait cards, a double-sided poster, art cards for each pinup, and 11 highly detailed hard plastic miniatures.

Each box makes 11 miniatures and contains:
3 Monster trait cards with new artwork.  These can be used as a fun and illustrative replacement for the trait cards in a monster's respective AI deck. This includes Baller and Separation Anxiety for the Dung Beetle Knight Expansion and Tombstone for the Manhunter Expansion.

The Arcane Secrets Expansion will transform how you play Runes of Zun. This expansion is all about advanced knowledge, fortune, and card manipulation.

There are six new action types (good and bad), a new reaction type, and a new and unique modifier type. Mix them into your original game for a total of 78 cards. If you also mix in the original Action Packed Expansion and the Shadow Tactics Expansion, you'll get a total of 114 cards in your deck (or 123 with all the promos!)

New tactics will emerge, and game-changing combos will win out. Are you ready?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Game of Life: A Day at the Dog Park
description:
Hit the dog park and share the adventures and surprises of dogs at play! The Game of Life: A Day at the Dog Park pet edition board game is a dog lover's version of classic The Game of Life gameplay. In this game it's all about the treats that are used to buy Dog Bones. Players can get them by landing on a Dog Bone or Steal a Dog Bone space, pulling an Action card, winning Spin to Win, or paying the baker.

—description from the publisher

Players play alternately. They use their deck of cards made up of saints, buildings and prayer cards.
There is also a common group of cards that bring negative points and they are activated as soon as the condition is met - sins.
By activating the blessings of the saints as well as by fulfilling the conditions of the buildings and prayers, points are collected. The first player to collect 100 points wins.

—user summary

"Orthodox Heroes" is a new social card game. The goal of this game is to achieve faith and get acquainted with the Orthodox elements. For all ages.

—description from the designer (translated)

"Православни Хероји" су нова друштвена картична игра. Циљ ове игре је постизање вере и упознавање са православнм елементима. За све узрасте.
—description from the designer

Quiz game with tricky questions and answers. E.g. "Are there more camels in China than in Egypt? The guessing person has to decide if it is right or wrong and then you read out the interesting answer. The person who gets the most 'right' wins.

—user summary

A trivia game with 80 questions about curious records. A game leader reads out the questions and the other players answer simultaneously. Whoever is correct receives one of the 30 score cards. After 30 point cards have been distributed, the player with the most points wins.

—user summary

From the outside, other Necromundans see House Cawdor as bonepickers, scavengers and beggars, little better than hive rats. But House Cawdor has faith, a faith so strong that it has been saved from extinction where so many other Clan Houses have succumbed to the march of time or other disasters.

Get the ultimate guide to House Cawdor. This 136-page hardback book features the definitive history of the House and rules for fielding the eight different types of fighters available to House Cawdor – Word-Keepers, Priests, Firebrands, Deacons, Cawdor Brethren, Redemptionist Brethren, Bonepickers, and Zealots. It's ideal for fans of House Cawdor or anyone who wants to delve deeper into the dark side of the underhive.

Inside this book you will find:


     House Cawdor History and Background: An expansive and extensive background on the origins of House Cawdor and how it went on to become the largest clan on Necromunda.
     House Cawdor Gang List: All the rules you need in order to field a Cawdor Gang in games of Necromunda, including rules for using Redemptionists in your gangs or for fielding gangs made entirely of these flaming zealots.
     Hangers-on and Brutes: Rules for hiring Hangers-on and Brutes for your Gang, including Cawdor-specific ones and for using them in games of Necromunda.
     Hired Guns: Rules for using Bounty Hunters, House Agents, and Hive Scum.
     Strong Alliances: Rules for forming alliances with three organisations that House Cawdor has connections with, including the Corpse Guild, Rogue Factoria, and House Ko'iron – the Noble House that backs the House of Faith.
     Additional Rules: This section includes new and additional rules usable by House Cawdor gangs in games of Necromunda, providing Cawdor players a variety of options, including Skills, Abilities, and gang-specific terrain.
     Weapon Reference Chart: A comprehensive weapons reference chart for all weapons and wargear available to House Cawdor and their Allies.
     House Cawdor Gang Tactics: A D66 table of Gang Tactics for use in Scenarios.
     Dramatis Personae: Rules for hiring and using four brand-new Dramatis Personae characters in your games of Necromunda.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: On the Rocks: Promo Drink Cards
description: 40 new drink recipes with rainbow ingredient spaces. Each recipe has two wild spaces, which are easier to fulfil, but earns $1 less than usual.

Follow the stories of one of the most famous Nordic heroes, Ragnar Lothbrok, his wife, the shield maid Lagertha and his brother Rollo, along with many other characters.

Enter the mysterious world of the Northmen: war, power and fraternity. You can become one of them in this game!

Choose one of the special characters, collect your favourite characters and kingdoms and build boats and longships. The Odin and Valhalla cards can bring blessings or curse. But be careful! In order not to displease the gods, you will have to make sacrifices.

Are you ready for this adventure.

The Fallen are summoned to the battle and the underworld is disturbed. The fallen titan of a past age wakes up from the other side and stands to fight his last battle. He has no obligation to one Nation, but his eternal reason – as for any titan – is to wage war. He will move from army to army, sowing terror in the hearts of his enemies.

The Fallen Titan has the same size scale to the other Titans and the miniature is 6.7 cm tall because of his pose.

boardgame: Dinosaur Table Battles: More Dinosaurs, Please
description:
This is an expansion for Dinosaur Table Battles. What happened is that Mary asked for more dinosaurs – she may or may not have said please, and it may or may not have been a request. Regardless, six new dino-combatants are ready to join the fifteen from the base game.

Some of the new dinos are classified as Air Dinos and some as Wet Dinos. Air Dinos ignore blocks – unless those blocks are thrown by other Air Dinos! Likewise, the targeting restrictions of Wet Dinos can be negated by enemy Wet Dinos. Air Dinos and Wet Dinos are also weaker against each other – Air Dinos aren't very strong swimmers, and Wet Dinos are afraid of heights. From the massive Quetzalcoatlus to the cunning Gallimimus, these new dinosaurs can dramatically change the flow of a match.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Horrors arise. Your Investigators are the people on the spot. If you can’t stop the Menace now, the city, or even the world, may be doomed. You are the only hope, if you can withstand the terrors and stress of the frights that await you.

Terror Paths is a tabletop cooperative experience for several players. All players take on the role of Investigators, who work together as a team. The game comes with two maps, each with wildly different scenarios. While you can just play a one-off scenario, you can also play the game as a campaign.  Keep an eye out for more Terror Paths maps, scenarios, and campaigns.

All the investigators can move and take actions simultaneously, while they puzzle out arcane mysteries, gain insight, and find the tools they need to stave off the Menace. As the adventure begins, you don’t know how to defeat your opposition or even what that opposition consists of!

After the investigators take their turn, the Menace advances. Threat increases, Doom rises, and terrible events occur. Keep in mind that the game has a time limit–if you delay too much, you are ruined. Draw back the veil and embark upon the Terror Path!

—description from the publisher

Immerse yourself in ArtMASTERS-The Game: A fun, challenging competition between 2 to 4 Players ages 14+ to create famous paintings by famous artists one tile at a time! You don't have to be an artist, or even need to know a lot about art. Your game board Canvas is your guide. With each tile drawn, every Player can be an accomplished artist as a Masterpiece is revealed! It's all in the Tube.

How ArtMASTERS is Played

The Object of the Game is to be the first to complete a Masterwork painting in tiles on your game board, call Frame It! and score. 2 to 4 can play.

You don't have to be an artist, or need to know a lot about art. Your game board Canvas is your guide.

Draw Game tiles from the Tube.
Decide which of three paintings to re-create on your game board Canvas.
Tile-by-tile create a famous artist's Masterpiece.
Be the first to complete a 3x4 inch painting in 12 tiles and call Frame It!  to score.
Only the Player who calls Frame It!  for a completed painting scores for that round.

The Game ends when a Player reaches 500 points.

—description from the publisher

It’s round about 4 billion years ago. Earth has just recently cooled off enough to have water on its surface. Impacts of comets and meteorites have almost ceased. The sky is orange and dense with thick clouds, through which a faint Sun barely shines. A vast, greenish, muddy ocean covers almost the entire planet. The little dry land is black volcanic rocks and barren. But deep in the ocean and in a few oases, something is cooking: life is about to emerge.

In AEON, you and your friends represent prebiotic environments where Life might have emerged. Together, you build a network of chemical reactions that transforms simple prebiotic reactants into complex molecules. Eventually, these molecules will allow you to establish the basic mechanisms of life which make the reactions more efficient.
The first player to create all the requirements for the emergence of biological evolution wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – New Vegas Rules Expansion
description:
Enter the dangerous world of the Mojave wasteland, Fight over its future at the Hoover Dam, and get some R and R on the lush New Vegas Strip – if you can afford the entry fee!

The Mojave wasteland stretches from New California Territory to the contested Hoover Dam, containing all sorts of treasures for the bold and dangers for the unwary. For those who come out on tip there is glory or territory to be gained; the unlucky join the desert sands of feed the geckos.

The risks are high, but the rewards are great, all within sight of the glowing lights of New Vegas!

This essential rules expansion allows you to add the flora, fauna and characters from New Vegas to your games of Wasteland Warfare. The New Vegas Rules Expansion includes cards for playing with new Fallout: Wasteland Warfare sets, as well as rules and scenarios for playing in the Mojave wasteland.

The accompanying 40-page rulebook includes a guide to building and painting your NCR and Legion models, a miniatures gallery, new rules for storyline events, a five mission campaign allowing you to refight the battle for the Hoover Dam (with the faction of your choice) and mini achievements to try to earn in games.

Faction/model type rules included in the set cover the NCR, Caesar’s Legion and the Super Mutant Nightkin as well as key characters and allies for the Survivors.

Includes 81 Poker and 150 Euro mini pop-out cards.

In Spy Sabotage, you enter a secret underground auction where the goal is simple: Outbid your opponents in order to collect the three items you need to complete your mission. However, there will be other spies surrounding you, willing to do whatever it takes to obtain the items needed for their own missions. Place your bids strategically and sabotage your opponents when the moment is right!

Spy Sabotage is a fast-paced strategy game for 2-6 players. At the beginning of the game, each player receives a Mission card that shows them the 3 items they need to collect to win the game. Each player also receives the limited funds they will use to collect those items. Each round of the game, players will silently bid on the items available with the goal of collecting their three items, while preventing their opponents from collecting the items they need. Each player also has one Sabotage they can use each game, which destroys an item as well as any bids placed on the item. The first player to collect all three of their items wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns moving around the swimming pool playing board collecting one of each of the coloured rings to stack on their 3D llama. Then race to be the first to get your llama to dive into the swimming pool to win the fame!

A player draws a Black card and reads it aloud. Then, all players choose a Red card from their hand that would fill the |...| of the Black card. To make their Red card more interesting, all players choose 1 sticker and stick it on the Red card. As soon as everyone is ready, the creations are revealed in turn. As soon as everyone is ready, all players point to their favorite Red card. The player with the most votes wins the card.

Create funny or surprising combinations to fill in the |...| of the Black card. With the stickers your game will become unique and full of epic memories! Finally a communication game for the whole family.

In this game you are Barbie. A well-known travel company offers you the opportunity to go around the world to collect documentation on the cities visited. A prize is awarded to you for each documentation. You will win if you reach $ 100,000 first, as long as you have at least one ticket for each of the 6 continents.

The players draw 10 letters from a bag with which they must be able to compose the name of a city they want to reach. If they succeed, they move their marker to the city and are rewarded with $ (depending on the city), otherwise they draw another letter and pass the turn to the next player.

Seesaw is a Draughts-inspired elimination game, played on a hexhex board of size 4 or 5. Each player needs, in their own color, a large number of stackable tokens and tiles.

Rules

Overview:
In Seesaw the players command opposing armies of soldiers that can promote. Your army starts out as a single soldier in the bottom corner. This soldier stands on a tile of your color. This tile is your initial promotion area, and it will evolve as you deploy more soldiers. Every time a soldier promotes it grows - first into a 2-stack, then a 3-stack, and so on.

The central idea of Seesaw is the 2-fold significance of stack-size: 
(i) Odd-sized stacks move forwards and even-sized stacks move backwards, and 
(ii) on any given turn, a stack can take as many steps as it likes from 1 up to its size.

This results in soldiers seesawing up and down the board as their strength increases to the point where the game is forced to conclude in one side completely eliminating the other.

How to play:
On your turn, you must either move a stack, or place a new stack.

1. Placing a new stack: 
A newly placed stack will always be of size 1. You may only place a stack on an empty cell next to a cell with a tile of your color. These cells are promotion cells, and each time you place a new stack you first place a tile of your color on that cell. A cell with a tile on it is not considered empty, but tiles never count toward stacks-size.

2. Moving a stack:
You only ever move one stack per turn, but that stack may be able to move several steps or capture several enemy stacks, depending on its size.
The number of steps a stack may take is the number of pieces in it, i.e. its size. A stack may never step onto a cell containing a stack of its color.

2.1 Noncapturing moves: On turns where you do not have to capture an enemy (see below) all the steps a stack takes is either constrained to the 3 upward directions ("forwards") or to the 3 downward directions ("backwards"), depending on the parity of the size of the stack. Up and down have their standard meanings for games where players sit on opposite sides of a board, like in Draughts and Chess.

2.2 Capturing moves: If one or more of your stacks can reach an enemy by lifting the restraint on direction, you must capture such a reachable enemy by replacement. If that stack did not spend all its steps reaching the captured enemy, you can, before you end your turn, spend the left-over steps by making a noncapturing move. However, if yet an enemy is in range immediately after you have captured (counting only the steps left over), you must capture again.

3. Promoting: Your odd-sized stacks promote when they end up on a promotion cell belonging to your opponent at the end of your turn. Your even-sized stacks promote when they end up on a promotion cell belonging to you at the end of your turn. When a stack promotes, you add 1 piece to it.

The object of Seesaw is to capture all your opponent's soldiers. More precisely: If, at the end of your turn, your opponent has no stacks on the board, you have won the game.

Goal of the game: to be the only person with no cards remaining in front of you. If the players believes that their card dates from BEFORE the initial card, they place their card to the LEFT of the initial card, if they believes that their card dates from AFTER the initial card, they place their card to the RIGHT of the initial card.

Bring to the table the inscrutable force of fate through a set collection mechanic. Make the Omen come true by collecting signs, sacrificing immediate gains to unravel treacherous powers.

The Omen is a new type of card which comes along with its own several abilities. An Omen card allows you to beat your opponents by building a long term alternative strategy that could be a game changer, if deployed in the right moment. This kind of strategy is not costless, but it surely leads to huge rewards.

The new Omen cards provide powerful effects that need specific set collections to trigger. The cards collected in this way are called Signs and can be added or removed during the game, offering new opportunities for direct interaction between players.

Volfyirion Guilds - Omen Expansion is not a standalone game. 
It can be played either with Volfyirion, or Volfyirion Guilds in 1vs1 mode.

The Omen Expansion can be combined with Volfyirion, Volfyirion Guilds, or any other expansion, for an even more challenging experience. In any case we do not recommend combining more than two expansions at the same time.

—description from the publisher

Accomplish your Guild's hidden agenda through low blows and tricky interruptions during the opponent's turn that will increase the complexity and tension of the game.

The Ploy is a new type of card which comes along with its own several abilities. 
A Ploy Card, with its asymmetric and secret new strategy, may allow you to beat your opponent, overwriting the rules of the game in specific conditions.

The new Ploy cards provide alternative ways to interact with the opponent, even during their turn, to sabotage their strategies when needed. A Ploy card must remain face down until they are played, but Ploys can also be drawn, forcefully revealed, swapped, or removed by the effect of other abilities during the game.

Volfyirion Guilds Ploy Expansion is not a standalone game. 
It can be played either with Volfyirion, or Volfyirion Guilds in 1vs1 mode. 
The Ploy Expansion can be combined with Volfyirion, Volfyirion Guilds, or any other expansion, for an even more challenging experience. In any case we do not recommend combining more than two expansions at the same time.

—description from the publisher

Mity is a simple territory game of pure placement. It is played on the cells of a hexhex board.

Rules

Players place one token of their color each turn and the first player unable to do so has lost. You may place on any empty cell unless your opponent has a token closer to that cell than you have along at least 2 of the 3 lines (axes) the cell is on.

The game uses the pie rule: One player chooses the first move and the other player chooses whose move it is.

—description from the designer

Garner is a combinatorial card game for two players where you seek to collect a majority of cards in a majority of suits. It is played with a deck of cards containing 5 suits of 15 numbers each.

Setup:
Shuffle the deck and fan out 25 stacks of 3 cards each in a regular layout of 5 rows and 5 columns. All cards should be visible, and it should be clear which cards are on which layer of the layout. (The 25 top cards in the initial position are on the top layer, and underneath that there are two more layers of 25 cards each.)

Definitions:
Every card belongs in a number group together with all and only those cards which are connected to it by a sequence of orthogonal steps between cards with equal numbers. The members of a group all lie on the same layer.
A suit group is defined the same way, except "number" is replaced with "suit".

Play:
On your turn, you first distribute a number group, then you collect a suit group.

Distribute: Pick up a number group from the current top layer and distribute them anew on the layout. You may not place two of these cards in the same stack, and you may not place them underneath any cards. Other than that, you are free to choose any of the 25 sites in the layout. There is no limit on how many cards there can be at each site.

Collect: Pick up any suit group from the current top layer and store those cards in a personal scoring pile on your side of the board.

Ending the game:
When all cards are collected, the winner is the player whose scoring pile contains the majority of the cards in the majority of the suits.

Slight variations: 

     Add the requirement that all cards (or at least one card) in the chosen number group are in a new position after distributing.
     When distributing, cards must be moved in a straight (diagonal or orthogonal) line.


Decks you can play Garner with:
Rage
Stick 'Em
5°Dimension Playing Card Game
Sluff Off!
Tricky Bid
David & Goliath

Ways of playing with more traditional decks:

(A) 

     9 cards in 3 suits from a standard French deck. 
     Layout: 9 stacks of 3 cards each in a 3x3 grid.


(B) 

     12 cards in 3 suits from a standard French deck. 
     Layout: 9 stacks of 4 cards each in a 3x3 grid. 
     Extra rule: You may never have exactly 6 cards in a suit in your scoring pile. If collecting a suit group would bring the number of cards you have in that suit to 6, you must leave behind a card in the layout, even if it means collecting 0 cards.


(C) 

     13 cards in 3 suits and 9 cards in 1 suit from a standard French deck. 
     Layout: 16 stacks of 3 cards each in a 4x4 grid. 
     Extra rule: The suit with 9 cards is worth half a suit.


(D) 

     9 cards in 4 suits from a standard French deck and 9 cards in a suit form a different deck, like a German deck or Tarot deck. 
     Layout: 15 stacks of 3 cards each in a 3x5 grid.


(E)

     12 cards in 4 suits from a standard French deck and 12 cards in a suit form a different deck, like a German deck or Tarot deck.
     Layout: 20 stacks of 3 cards each in a 4x5 grid.
     Extra rule: You may never have exactly 6 cards in a suit in your scoring pile. If collecting a suit group would bring the number of cards you have in that suit to 6, you must leave behind a card in the layout, even if it means collecting 0 cards.


—description from the designer

Bowline is played on a hexhex board with stones of two colors, one color belonging to each player. The winner of the game is the player to first make a loop consisting entirely of stones of their color.

Rules:
On your turn, you must either (i) place a stone of your color on an empty hex, or (ii) flip an eligible arc of enemy stones. You may not do both.

Definitions:
To flip an enemy stone is to replace it with a stone of your own, from your (preferably endless) supply.

An arc is a possibly bent line of stones with no acute angles and no turns going in opposite directions (no S-like swingyness). (Se below for a more precise definition of an arc.)

You are said to flank an arc of enemy pieces if two of your pieces  are the endpoints of an arc otherwise completely made up of said enemy pieces.

You may flip an arc of enemy pieces if you are the only one flanking it.

A loop is a group of stones causing one or more cells not contained in that group to be severed from the edges of the board in this sense: There is no chain of adjacent cells going from any of those severed cells to any edge cell that does not contain at least one stone from the loop group. (It's a curve of stones biting itself in the ass, around at least one enemy stone or at least one empty cell.)

More precise definition of an arc:
A chain of adjacent pieces make up an arc if it is possible to order them from first to last in a sequence where a walk from the first to the last member of the chain, visiting all members in order, will (i) at no point require a turn of more than 60 degrees, and (ii) either require no clockwise turns or no counter-clockwise turns.

—description from the designer

Unknot is played on a hexhex board oriented so that there is a horizontal line of adjacent cells between one pair of two opposing corner cells.

Both players share a pool of stones of a single color and take turns placing then onto empty cells.

Placement restrictions.

     The center cell is unplayable throughout the game. 
     Player 1 may not place immediately northwest of a stone. 
     Player 2 may not place immediately northeast of a stone.


The game is over as soon as one player completes a loop of pieces around at least one empty cell. This player is then the winner. The game cannot end in a draw, so can also be played with the opposite goal: The player who completes a loop loses.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Remigio, lungo viaggio alla ricerca della mamma
description:
The players play the role of Remigio, a child in search of his mother. They will have to reach various places on the map where they can discover clues that will lead them to discover the identity of their mother, hidden at the beginning of the game in a secret place. In the various places visited they will be able to acquire objects that will allow them to move faster, to be the first to discover the identity of their mother and thus win the game.
The game is evidently inspired by the cartoon television series Remi, broadcast in Italy for the first time in 1979

Hoop is played on a hexhex board of size 4-6. Each player has a pool of stones in their own color in addition to a pool of shared neutral stones.

Rules:
Each turn, you first place a neutral stone on an empty cell or replace one of your stones with a neutral stone. Then, you place a stone of your color on an empty cell.
